name: Pear (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw European pear, Pyrus communis)

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Dominant sugar in pear; USDA fructose value, consistent with P. communis cultivar mean ~46 g/kg FW (Conference 6.6, Williams 7.2 g/100g).

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: USDA glucose value; cultivar mean ~13 g/kg FW.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Lowest of the free sugars in pear; cultivar mean ~6 g/kg FW.

components.name: D-sorbitol
components.source: Aprifel (Conference 1.8, Williams 1.9 g/100g); Radovic et al. 2022 PMC9563278 (mean ~18 g/kg FW)
components.evidence: Sorbitol is a signature fermentable polyol of Pyrus (Rosaceae); a major growth-relevant carbon source usually absent from USDA total-sugar panels.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Minor free sugar reported in USDA panel.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Radovic et al. 2022 PMC9563278 (mean ~1.2 g/kg FW); Zhang et al. 2022 doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2022.104481 (mean 1.69 mg/g FW)
components.evidence: Predominant organic acid; P. communis is a malic-acid-dominant type. A key non-sugar carbon/energy source, omitted from the FooDB profile.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Radovic et al. 2022 PMC9563278 (mean ~0.5 g/kg FW); Zhang et al. 2022 doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2022.104481
components.evidence: Second organic acid; higher in Asian pears (citric-dominant type), moderate in European pear.

components.name: quinic acid
components.source: Zhang et al. 2022 (193 Pyrus cultivars) doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2022.104481 (mean 1.05 mg/g FW)
components.evidence: Third-ranked organic acid in pear; growth-relevant and missing from the current profile.

components.name: shikimic acid
components.source: Radovic et al. 2022 PMC9563278; Chen et al. 2007 (Pyrus organic acids)
components.evidence: Minor organic acid consistently detected in pear (0.02-0.15 g/kg FW).

components.name: fumaric acid
components.source: Radovic et al. 2022 PMC9563278 (0.001-0.01 g/kg FW)
components.evidence: Trace organic acid; included for completeness of the TCA-linked pool.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Low protein content typical of pome fruit.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: By far the dominant free/protein amino acid in pear.

components.name: vitamin A (RAE, as provitamin carotenoids)
components.evidence: Near-trace; from beta-carotene/lutein, not preformed retinol.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid of the (very low) pear lipid fraction.

components.name: arbutin
components.source: FooDB FOOD00152; Cui et al. 2005 (Pyrus arbutin)
components.evidence: Hydroquinone-beta-D-glucoside, a signature pear phenolic glycoside; present but not quantified here.

components.name: chlorogenic acid (5-caffeoylquinic acid)
components.evidence: Dominant hydroxycinnamate of pear; genuine phytochemical retained (no BiGG metabolite).

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic non-nutritive contaminant element, not a nutrient

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Lead
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Mercury
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: ultratrace element; only nutritionally relevant via B12 which is absent in this plant food; FooDB noise

remove.name: Lactose C12H22O11
remove.reason: milk disaccharide, biologically implausible in a plant fruit; FooDB artifact

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: preformed (animal) vitamin A; plants supply provitamin-A carotenoids, not retinol; implausible in pear

remove.name: Gadoleic acid
remove.reason: C20:1 fatty acid not a real constituent of pear lipid; FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Barium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Strontium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Titanium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Zirconium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Vanadium
remove.reason: non-nutritive ultratrace/contaminant element

remove.name: Lithium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Rubidium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: microbial-fermentation food preservative, not a native pear constituent; FooDB artifact

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae/citrus, biologically implausible in pear; FooDB predicted

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: predicted non-biological stereoisomer, analytical/predicted artifact

missing_added: individual free sugars with real amounts (fructose 6.42, glucose 2.60, sucrose 0.71 g/100g)
vitamin K1, choline, pantothenic acid, selenium, fluoride quantified
keep_uncovered: procyanidins B1, B2, B3, B7, C1 and trimers
boron, silicon (plant micronutrients, no BiGG metabolite)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169118 (Pears, raw; SR Legacy full nutrient panel via nutritionvalue.org)
Radovic et al. 2022, Foods, PMC9563278 (sugars & organic acids of 10 Pyrus communis cultivars)
Zhang et al. 2022, J. Food Composition and Analysis, doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2022.104481 (organic acids in 193 Pyrus cultivars)
Aprifel nutritional sheets (Conference & Williams pear individual sugars incl. sorbitol)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbe growing on pear are fructose (~6.4 g/100g) then sorbitol (~1.8 g/100g) and glucose (~2.6 g/100g), with malic acid (~0.12 g/100g) the leading organic acid. Sorbitol and all organic acids were absent from the FooDB profile and were the main growth-relevant additions; 21 non-nutritive contaminants/implausible compounds were removed.
